ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Take a look at the solar system with this [interactive map](https://www.solarsystemscope.com/).
[ ] Check out these [skywatching tips](https://science.nasa.gov/skywatching/) from NASA and go skywatching on a clear night.
[ ] Set up a simple weather station to watch through the year.

[ ] Create a [simple closed terrarium](https://climatekids.nasa.gov/mini-garden/) to watch the water cycle continuously.
[ ] You can also create a [water cycle in a bag](https://www.sciencemuseumok.org/smoathome/try-see-water-cycle-bag).
[ ] Play a [guessing game](https://www.kids-pages.com/folders/worksheets/Nature/SeasonsGuess.pdf) giving hints one at a time about which season you are describing. Try to guess the answer with the least number of hints. Here are some sample clues.

[ ] This [National Geographic page](https://education.nationalgeographic.org/resource/maps-and-models/) has information on maps of familiar places along with simple questions to explore those maps.
[ ] Print or copy a world map and take it with you to a zoo. Mark which continent each animal lives in on your map.
[ ] Choose favorite picture books from your shelves or the library and find the character's home on a map. Suggestions include Paddington Bear, Anna Hibiscus, Madeline, etc.
